title: galium palustre var. humile
intro: Galium palustre var. humile, also known as low cleavers, is a perennial herb in the bedstraw family native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Galium palustre var. humile include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or tincture that can be used to alleviate various health issues.
